title: ESP Security Rule Commit Without Approved Change Record

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NERC CIP CIP-005-6 R1 Part 1.4 is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.13.20: ESP Security Rule Commit Without Approved Change Record.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.13.20' running on index=network sourcetype=pan:config, index=itsm sourcetype=chg:firewall, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
spl:
index=network sourcetype=pan:config earliest=-7d
| where match(cmd,"(?i)commit")
| join type=left max=0 admin [
    search index=itsm sourcetype=chg:firewall earliest=-30d
    | stats latest(change_id) as change_id by submitter
    | rename submitter as admin
  ]
| where isnull(change_id)
| table _time, admin, object, cmd, change_id
description: We flag firewall commits affecting ESP rules when no linked change ticket exists, supporting controlled configuration of the security perimeter.
value: We flag firewall commits affecting ESP rules when no linked change ticket exists, supporting controlled configuration of the security perimeter.
implementation: (1) Map Panorama admin IDs to ITSM submitter IDs. (2) Require change_id in commit messages where supported. (3) Weekly governance report. (4) Escalate repeat offenders.
visualization: Table (unapproved commits), Timeline, Bar chart (by admin)
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t latest(All_Changes.status) as agg_value from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - agg_value

knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We flag firewall commits affecting ESP rules when no linked change ticket exists, supporting controlled configuration of the security perimeter ‚Äî so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=network` `sourcetype=pan:config`, `index=itsm` `sourcetype=chg:firewall`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Map Panorama admin IDs to ITSM submitter IDs. (2) Require change_id in commit messages where supported. (3) Weekly governance report. (4) Escalate repeat offenders.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=network sourcetype=pan:config earliest=-7d
| where match(cmd,"(?i)commit")
| join type=left max=0 admin [
    search index=itsm sourcetype=chg:firewall earliest=-30d
    | stats latest(change_id) as change_id by submitter
    | rename submitter as admin
  ]
| where isnull(change_id)
| table _time, admin, object, cmd, change_id
```

Understanding this SPL

**ESP Security Rule Commit Without Approved Change Record (CIP-005-6 R1 Part 1.4)** ‚Äî We flag firewall commits affecting ESP rules when no linked change ticket exists, supporting controlled configuration of the security perimeter.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=network` `sourcetype=pan:config`, `index=itsm` `sourcetype=chg:firewall`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: network; **sourcetype**: pan:config. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=network, sourcetype=pan:config, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where match(cmd,"(?i)commit")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Joins to a subsearch with `join` ‚Äî set `max=` to match cardinality and avoid silent truncation.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where isnull(change_id)` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **ESP Security Rule Commit Without Approved Change Record (CIP-005-6 R1 Part 1.4)**): table _time, admin, object, cmd, change_id

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(All_Changes.status) as agg_value from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - agg_value
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**ESP Security Rule Commit Without Approved Change Record (CIP-005-6 R1 Part 1.4)** ‚Äî We flag firewall commits affecting ESP rules when no linked change ticket exists, supporting controlled configuration of the security perimeter.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=network` `sourcetype=pan:config`, `index=itsm` `sourcetype=chg:firewall`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Change.All_Changes` ‚Äî enable acceleration for that model.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (unapproved commits), Timeline, Bar chart (by admin)
